name: Anki Vector
short_description: A small expressive home companion robot with a cloud voice assistant, a camera, and an animated face that lives on a desk and reacts to people around it.
long_description: Vector is a palm-sized desktop robot from Anki (the studio behind the earlier Cozmo) that pairs Pixar-style character animation with practical AI: a Qualcomm processor, an HD camera, a time-of-flight laser, cliff and touch sensors, and a four-microphone array let it map and roam a tabletop via SLAM, recognize faces and voices, answer questions, and emote through a high-resolution color 'eye' display. It is a clear case of physical AI as social character rather than tool: most of its design effort goes into legible, affective behavior so it reads as a tiny living companion. Anki shut down in April 2019 after failing to secure funding; later that year its assets, including Vector and Cozmo, were acquired by Digital Dream Labs, which kept the cloud servers running and shipped a Vector 2.0.

images.caption: An Anki Vector home companion robot on a desk.
images.alt_text: A small dark grey treaded robot with an illuminated rectangular blue-eyed face display, sitting on a wooden surface.

sources.title: Vector - ROBOTS: Your Guide to the World of Robotics
sources.access_notes: Specifications: processor, sensors, camera, microphone array, display, SLAM navigation.

sources.title: Anki (American company)
sources.access_notes: Company history, Cozmo (2016) and Vector (2018) launches, April 2019 shutdown.

sources.title: Anki assets acquired by edtech startup Digital Dream Labs
sources.access_notes: Reports December 2019 acquisition of Anki assets (Overdrive, Cozmo, Vector) by Digital Dream Labs.

interactions.title: Rub its back until it purrs
interactions.description: Stroke the touch-sensitive strip on Vector's back and its eyes narrow into a contented squint while it coos; grab and lift it instead and it tenses up like a startled pet.

interactions.title: Offer a fist and get a bump back
interactions.description: Say 'Hey Vector, give me a fist bump' and hold out a fist; the robot raises its lift arm to meet it and reacts to the contact.

interactions.title: A desk timer that watches you back
interactions.description: Ask 'Hey Vector, set a timer for 5 minutes' or 'what's the weather?' and it answers aloud, then goes back to roaming the desk, recognizing your face when it rolls past.

interactions.title: It hunts down its own cube to play with
interactions.description: Left alone, Vector wanders off to find its companion cube, shoves it around, and pops a little stunt with it; you can also say 'Hey Vector, find your cube' to send it looking.

interactions.title: Owners kept it alive after the company died
interactions.description: When Anki folded and the cloud servers were at risk, the community wrote wire-pod, a free server you run on a Raspberry Pi so a production Vector keeps its voice commands working with no subscription and no connection to the original company.

interactions.title: Reprogrammed into a tiny SDK lab pet
interactions.description: Tinkerers use the Python SDK to drive Vector's camera, SLAM map, and animation engine directly, scripting custom reactions and home-and-classroom projects far beyond the stock voice commands.

interactions.title: Greets the person it learned by name
interactions.description: Having enrolled your face, Vector could perk up and play a personalized animation the moment it rolls into view of a recognized owner versus a stranger, using the face recognition and expressive display it already has.

interactions.title: An edge-of-desk lookout
interactions.description: With its cliff sensors and front-facing camera, Vector could patrol the rim of a workspace and chirp a warning when an object (a mug, a phone) drifts toward the edge, leaning on the same hazard sensing that keeps it from driving off the table.
